message:
feat: Add Git LFS for model files

- Configure Git LFS to track *.gguf, *.lora, *.bin, *.safetensors
- Add Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf (4.4GB)
- Add Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf (12GB)
- Update .gitignore to allow LFS-tracked files
- Document model setup and Git LFS usage in MODELS.md

This enables version control of large model files using Git LFS,
making it easier to share and track model versions across the team.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Git LFS configuration for Zell
+
+# Track model files with Git LFS
+*.gguf filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
+
+# Track LoRA adapter files
+*.lora filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
+
+# Track other large binary files
+*.bin filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
+*.safetensors filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text

@@ -11,10 +11,10 @@ zig-out/
 *.dll
 *.exe
 
-# Models (don't commit large files)
-*.gguf
-*.bin
-models/
+# Models (tracked by Git LFS - see .gitattributes)
+# *.gguf - now tracked by Git LFS
+# *.bin - now tracked by Git LFS
+# models/ - now tracked by Git LFS
 
 # IDE
 .vscode/

@@ -0,0 +1,168 @@
+# Model Files
+
+This repository uses Git LFS (Large File Storage) to manage model files.
+
+## Available Models
+
+### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct (Q4_K_M)
+- **Size:** 4.4GB
+- **File:** `models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`
+- **Description:** 7B parameter coding model, quantized to Q4_K_M for efficient inference
+- **Best for:** Code generation, code understanding, general programming tasks
+- **Context:** 32K tokens
+
+### Codestral-22B (Q4_K_M)
+- **Size:** 12GB
+- **File:** `models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf`
+- **Description:** 22B parameter coding model, quantized to Q4_K_M
+- **Best for:** Complex code generation, architectural decisions, larger context needs
+- **Context:** Varies by version
+
+## Setup Instructions
+
+### First-time Setup
+
+1. **Install Git LFS:**
+   ```bash
+   # macOS
+   brew install git-lfs
+
+   # Ubuntu/Debian
+   sudo apt-get install git-lfs
+
+   # Windows
+   # Download from https://git-lfs.github.com/
+   ```
+
+2. **Initialize Git LFS in your repository:**
+   ```bash
+   git lfs install
+   ```
+
+3. **Clone the repository (this will automatically download LFS files):**
+   ```bash
+   git clone https://github.com/yourusername/zell.git
+   cd zell
+   ```
+
+### If You Already Cloned Without LFS
+
+If you cloned before setting up Git LFS, pull the actual model files:
+
+```bash
+git lfs install
+git lfs pull
+```
+
+## Manual Download (Alternative)
+
+If you prefer not to use Git LFS or hit bandwidth limits, download models manually:
+
+### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct
+```bash
+# Create models directory
+mkdir -p models
+
+# Download from Hugging Face
+wget -O models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf
+```
+
+### Codestral-22B
+```bash
+# Download from Hugging Face
+wget -O models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf
+```
+
+## Usage
+
+Once you have the models, build and run:
+
+```bash
+# Build optimization profile for the model
+./zig-out/bin/zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
+
+# Run inference
+./zig-out/bin/zell run models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf "Write a hello world in Zig"
+
+# With system prompt caching (faster subsequent runs!)
+./zig-out/bin/zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  --system-prompt "You are a helpful Zig programming assistant."
+```
+
+## Storage Requirements
+
+- **Git LFS Storage:** ~16.4GB total for both models
+- **Disk Space:** ~16.4GB + cache (~1-2GB for profiles and system prompts)
+
+**Note:** GitHub Free tier provides:
+- 1GB LFS storage
+- 1GB/month bandwidth
+
+For repositories with these large models, consider:
+- **GitHub Pro** ($4/month) - 50GB storage, 50GB/month bandwidth
+- **External hosting** - Store models on Hugging Face and document download links
+- **Git LFS storage packs** - Purchase additional storage as needed
+
+## Adding New Models
+
+To add a new model to the repository:
+
+```bash
+# 1. Place the .gguf file in the models/ directory
+cp /path/to/new-model.gguf models/
+
+# 2. Git LFS will automatically track it (configured in .gitattributes)
+git add models/new-model.gguf
+
+# 3. Commit and push
+git commit -m "Add new model: new-model.gguf"
+git push
+```
+
+The `.gitattributes` file automatically tracks:
+- `*.gguf` - Model files
+- `*.lora` - LoRA adapter files
+- `*.bin` - Binary checkpoint files
+- `*.safetensors` - SafeTensors format files
+
+## Troubleshooting
+
+### "This exceeds GitHub's file size limit"
+You need to use Git LFS. Follow the setup instructions above.
+
+### "Git LFS bandwidth limit exceeded"
+Options:
+1. Wait for the monthly bandwidth reset
+2. Upgrade to GitHub Pro for more bandwidth
+3. Download models manually from Hugging Face
+4. Purchase additional LFS storage packs
+
+### Models not downloading during clone
+```bash
+# Ensure Git LFS is installed
+git lfs install
+
+# Pull LFS files manually
+git lfs pull
+```
+
+### Check LFS status
+```bash
+# See which files are tracked by LFS
+git lfs ls-files
+
+# See LFS storage usage
+git lfs env
+```
+
+## Performance Tips
+
+After downloading models:
+1. Build the model once: `zell build <model.gguf>`
+2. Use `--system-prompt` during build for faster subsequent runs
+3. Profiles are cached in `~/.cache/zell/profiles/`
+4. System prompt caches are in `~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/`
+
+See [BENCHMARKS.md](BENCHMARKS.md) for detailed performance analysis.
